verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease of the kallikrein family, synthesized as a prepro-protein, processed in the secretory pathway, and released into prostatic fluid and serum where it cleaves pro-PSA. It has no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no outer-leaflet lipidation. Walking the contextual buckets: (1) no documented cancer/cell-death/activation ecto-pool of the protein body — KLK2 is upregulated in prostate cancer but as a secreted product; (2) prostate-restricted expression but the protein is secreted, not surface-anchored, so tissue-restricted_surface does not apply; (3) not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) no documented PM minority pool or cycling; (5) no wash-resistant covalent/non-covalent tethering to a TM partner. Clinical interest (e.g., radioligand/CAR programs) targets PSMA, not surface KLK2; KLK2-directed agents engage the secreted enzyme.
